title: Llama Guard: Safety Classifier Models
concept: Models like Llama Guard are specialized classification networks trained to categorize prompts/responses into safety classes (e.g. self-harm, hate speech).

exercises.prompt: In machine learning, what is a 'classifier' model best at doing?

options.text: Sorting or categorizing data into groups
Searching the internet for news articles

exercises.explain: Classifier models are designed to categorize input data (like text or images) into predefined classes or labels.

cards.title: Who Guards the AI?
cards.body: How do we make sure AI models don't give dangerous, hateful, or harmful advice? Instead of just hoping the AI behaves, we use specialized 'bodyguard' models like Llama Guard to screen every message!

cards.title: What is Llama Guard?
cards.body: Llama Guard is a specialized safety classifier model. Instead of writing essays or stories, it is trained specifically to read prompts and responses, then categorize them into safety classes (like hate speech, harassment, or self-harm) to block harmful content.

cards.title: The Security Guard
cards.body: Think of Llama Guard as a specialized mall security guard standing at the entrance. Instead of doing everything a general store clerk does (like answering questions or selling products), the guard has one specific job: check if what someone is carrying matches a list of prohibited items, like weapons or spray paint, and block them if they do.

cards.title: Llama Guard in Action
cards.body: If a user inputs a prompt asking for instructions on how to build something dangerous, Llama Guard processes the text first. It recognizes the prompt violates the 'dangerous content' category and outputs a label like 'unsafe', stopping the main AI from answering it.

cards.title: Myth: Main AIs Guard Themselves
cards.body: Many believe that one massive AI model handles its own safety perfectly. In reality, developers often use separate, specialized classifier models like Llama Guard. This keeps the safety checks fast, objective, and easy to update without retraining the giant main model.

cards.title: Chatbot vs. Safety Guard
cards.body: See how a safety classifier simplifies the evaluation process compared to a general chatbot.

left.text: Tries to answer, chat, write stories, write code, and converse freely.

right.text: Only looks for policy violations and outputs simple safety labels.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary purpose of Llama Guard?

options.text: To correct grammar mistakes in coding files
To categorize prompts and responses into safety classes
To generate high-quality images from text

exercises.explain: Llama Guard is trained to detect specific safety policy violations (like harassment or self-harm) and flag them as safe or unsafe.

exercises.prompt: Llama Guard can evaluate both the user's prompt and the AI's generated response for safety.
exercises.explain: Llama Guard can check both the user's incoming input (prompt) and the AI's outgoing output (response) to ensure safety.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe Llama Guard's model type.
exercises.sentence: Llama Guard is a safety [blank] model designed to categorize text.
exercises.explain: Llama Guard is a classifier model because its job is to categorize text into safety labels, rather than generating creative text.

exercises.prompt: Which of these outputs looks like it came from a safety classifier model processing a toxic prompt?

options.text: \"Sure, here is a poem about cybersecurity...\"

exercises.explain: The third response shows a safety classifier outputting the safety status ('unsafe') followed by the specific policy code violated ('S3' for hate speech/harassment).

exercises.prompt: How does Llama Guard fit into an AI application to protect users?

options.text: It checks both the user's prompt and the AI's response for safety violations.
It rewrite toxic prompts into polite prompts automatically.
It replaces the main AI model entirely to save computer memory.
It teaches the main AI how to write computer code.

exercises.explain: Llama Guard acts as a protective shield on both sides of a conversation: it screens incoming user prompts before they reach the main AI, and checks outgoing AI responses before they are shown to the user.
